    <h1 {...{
      "id": "add-on-project-anatomy-guide"
    }}>{`Add-on Project Anatomy Guide`}</h1>
    <p>{`A comprehensive guide to understanding Adobe Express add-on project structure, file organization, and template selection.`}</p>
    <InlineAlert variant="info" slots="header, text1" mdxType="InlineAlert" />
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`New to add-ons?`}</strong></p>
    <p>{`Start with the `}<a parentName="p" {...{

    <h2 {...{
      "id": "understanding-the-basics"
    }}>{`Understanding the Basics`}</h2>
    <p>{`Every Adobe Express add-on has these essential files:`}</p>
    <ol>
      <li parentName="ol"><strong parentName="li"><inlineCode parentName="strong">{`manifest.json`}</inlineCode></strong>{` - Tells Adobe Express about your add-on`}</li>
      <li parentName="ol"><strong parentName="li"><inlineCode parentName="strong">{`index.html`}</inlineCode></strong>{` - Your add-on's user interface`}</li>
      <li parentName="ol"><strong parentName="li"><inlineCode parentName="strong">{`index.js`}</inlineCode></strong>{` - Your add-on's logic`}</li>
    </ol>
    <p>{`The complexity grows based on:`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Whether you need to create/modify document content (requires `}<a parentName="li" {...{
          "href": "../learn/platform_concepts/architecture.md#document-sandbox"
        }}>{`document sandbox`}</a>{`)`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Whether you use a build tool (webpack)`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Whether you use a UI framework (React) or TypeScript`}</li>
    </ul>
    <p>{`See the `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "../learn/platform_concepts/architecture.md"
      }}>{`Add-on Architecture Guide`}</a>{` for a deep dive into how these pieces work together.`}</p>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "the-simplest-add-on"
    }}>{`The Simplest Add-on`}</h2>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "plain-javascript-template-no-build"
    }}>{`Plain JavaScript Template (No Build)`}</h3>
    <p>{`This is the absolute simplest structure - no build tools, no document manipulation:`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-text"
      }}>{`my-addon/
├── src/
│   ├── index.html       # Your UI
│   ├── index.js         # Your UI logic
│   └── manifest.json    # Configuration
├── tsconfig.json        # For IDE support only
└── README.md
`}</code></pre>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`Characteristics:`}</strong></p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul">{`✅ No build process - files are used directly as-is`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`✅ Perfect for learning`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`✅ Great for simple UI-only add-ons`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`❌ Cannot create/modify document content`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`❌ No modern JavaScript features (no `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`JSX`}</inlineCode>{`, no imports beyond basic ES modules)`}</li>
    </ul>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`manifest.json:`}</strong></p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-json"
      }}>{`{
    "entryPoints": [{
        "type": "panel",
        "main": "index.html"
    }]
}
`}</code></pre>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`When to use:`}</strong>{` Simple tools like settings panels, calculators, reference guides, or anything that doesn't need to create shapes/text/images in the document.`}</p>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`Related guides:`}</strong></p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul"><a parentName="li" {...{
          "href": "./local_development/dev_tooling.md"
        }}>{`Development Tools`}</a>{` - CLI commands and local development setup`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><a parentName="li" {...{

        }}>{`Manifest Reference`}</a>{` - Complete manifest configuration guide`}</li>
    </ul>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "build-vs-no-build-templates"
    }}>{`Build vs. No-Build Templates`}</h2>
    <p>{`Understanding the difference between build and no-build templates is essential for working with add-on projects:`}</p>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "no-build-templates"
    }}>{`No-Build Templates`}</h3>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`Only two templates have no build process:`}</strong></p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul"><inlineCode parentName="li">{`javascript`}</inlineCode>{` (UI-only)`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><inlineCode parentName="li">{`javascript-with-document-sandbox`}</inlineCode>{` (with document manipulation)`}</li>
    </ul>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`Characteristics:`}</strong></p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Files in `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`src/`}</inlineCode>{` are used directly`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><inlineCode parentName="li">{`manifest.json`}</inlineCode>{` paths point directly to source files`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`No `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`webpack.config.js`}</inlineCode>{` file`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`No `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`npm run build`}</inlineCode>{` command needed for development`}</li>
    </ul>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`Document sandbox path in manifest:`}</strong></p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-json"
      }}>{`"documentSandbox": "sandbox/code.js"  // Full path to source file
`}</code></pre>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "build-templates-all-others"
    }}>{`Build Templates (All Others)`}</h3>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`These templates use webpack:`}</strong></p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul"><inlineCode parentName="li">{`swc-javascript`}</inlineCode>{` / `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`swc-javascript-with-document-sandbox`}</inlineCode></li>
      <li parentName="ul"><inlineCode parentName="li">{`swc-typescript`}</inlineCode>{` / `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`swc-typescript-with-document-sandbox`}</inlineCode></li>
      <li parentName="ul"><inlineCode parentName="li">{`react-javascript`}</inlineCode>{` / `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`react-javascript-with-document-sandbox`}</inlineCode></li>
      <li parentName="ul"><inlineCode parentName="li">{`react-typescript`}</inlineCode>{` / `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`react-typescript-with-document-sandbox`}</inlineCode></li>
    </ul>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`Characteristics:`}</strong></p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Files in `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`src/`}</inlineCode>{` are bundled to `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`dist/`}</inlineCode></li>
      <li parentName="ul"><inlineCode parentName="li">{`manifest.json`}</inlineCode>{` paths point to bundled output files`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Has `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`webpack.config.js`}</inlineCode>{` file`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Requires `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`npm run build`}</inlineCode>{` to generate `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`dist/`}</inlineCode>{` folder`}</li>
    </ul>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`Document sandbox path in manifest:`}</strong></p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-json"
      }}>{`"documentSandbox": "code.js"  // Just filename - webpack outputs to dist/code.js
`}</code></pre>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`Why the difference?`}</strong></p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul">{`No-build: `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`"sandbox/code.js"`}</inlineCode>{` → Adobe Express loads `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`src/sandbox/code.js`}</inlineCode>{` directly`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Build: `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`"code.js"`}</inlineCode>{` → Webpack bundles `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`src/sandbox/code.js`}</inlineCode>{` → outputs to `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`dist/code.js`}</inlineCode>{` → Adobe Express loads `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`dist/code.js`}</inlineCode></li>
    </ul>
    <InlineAlert variant="info" slots="text" mdxType="InlineAlert" />
    <p>{`For more on webpack configuration and build tools, see `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "../build/advanced-topics/frameworks-libraries-bundling.md"
      }}>{`Frameworks, Libraries and Bundling`}</a>{`.`}</p>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "adding-document-manipulation"
    }}>{`Adding Document Manipulation`}</h2>
    <p>{`When your add-on needs to create or modify document content (shapes, text, images), you need the `}<strong parentName="p">{`document sandbox`}</strong>{`.`}</p>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "what-changes"
    }}>{`What Changes?`}</h3>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "1-folder-structure"
    }}>{`1. Folder Structure`}</h4>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`Without document sandbox:`}</strong></p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-text"
      }}>{`src/
├── index.html
├── index.js          # All code here
└── manifest.json
`}</code></pre>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`With document sandbox:`}</strong></p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-text"
      }}>{`src/
├── index.html
├── manifest.json
├── ui/               # UI code moves here
│   ├── index.js
│   └── tsconfig.json
└── sandbox/          # Document code goes here
    ├── code.js
    └── tsconfig.json
`}</code></pre>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "2-manifest-configuration"
    }}>{`2. Manifest Configuration`}</h4>
    <p>{`Add the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`documentSandbox`}</inlineCode>{` property:`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-json"
      }}>{`{
    "entryPoints": [{
        "type": "panel",
        "main": "index.html",
        "documentSandbox": "sandbox/code.js"  // or "code.js" for build templates
    }]
}
`}</code></pre>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "3-code-split"
    }}>{`3. Code Split`}</h4>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`UI code (`}<inlineCode parentName="strong">{`ui/index.js`}</inlineCode>{`):`}</strong></p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-javascript"
      }}>{`import addOnUISdk from "https://express.adobe.com/static/add-on-sdk/sdk.js";

addOnUISdk.ready.then(async () => {
    // Get proxy to communicate with document sandbox
    const sandboxProxy = await addOnUISdk.instance.runtime.apiProxy("documentSandbox");

    // UI interactions
    document.getElementById("createBtn").addEventListener("click", async () => {
        await sandboxProxy.createRectangle();  // Call document sandbox
    });
});
`}</code></pre>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`Document sandbox code (`}<inlineCode parentName="strong">{`sandbox/code.js`}</inlineCode>{`):`}</strong></p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-javascript"
      }}>{`import addOnSandboxSdk from "add-on-sdk-document-sandbox";
import { editor } from "express-document-sdk";

const { runtime } = addOnSandboxSdk.instance;

runtime.exposeApi({
    createRectangle: () => {
        const rectangle = editor.createRectangle();
        rectangle.width = 240;
        rectangle.height = 180;
        editor.context.insertionParent.children.append(rectangle);
    }
});
`}</code></pre>

    <h2 {...{
      "id": "understanding-ui-options-swc-vs-react"
    }}>{`Understanding UI Options: SWC vs. React`}</h2>
    <p>{`When choosing a template with a build process, you have two main UI approaches: `}<strong parentName="p">{`SWC templates`}</strong>{` (vanilla JavaScript) or `}<strong parentName="p">{`React templates`}</strong>{` (React framework).`}</p>
    <InlineAlert slots="header, text1" variant="info" mdxType="InlineAlert" />
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`Important`}</strong></p>
    <p>{`Both template types use Spectrum Web Components by default. The key difference is `}<strong parentName="p">{`how you write your code`}</strong>{` (vanilla JavaScript with Lit vs. React with JSX), not which UI components you use.`}</p>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "what-are-swc-templates"
    }}>{`What are SWC Templates?`}</h3>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`SWC`}</strong>{` stands for `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "https://opensource.adobe.com/spectrum-web-components/"
      }}><strong parentName="a">{`Spectrum Web Components`}</strong></a>{` - Adobe's implementation of the Spectrum design system as web components.`}</p>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`SWC templates are characterized by:`}</strong></p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`Vanilla JavaScript`}</strong>{` (no UI framework like React)`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><a parentName="li" {...{
          "href": "https://lit.dev/docs/v1/lit-html/introduction/"
        }}><strong parentName="a">{`Lit`}</strong></a>{` for creating custom web components`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`Native Spectrum Web Components`}</strong>{` (`}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`<sp-button>`}</inlineCode>{`, `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`<sp-theme>`}</inlineCode>{`, etc.)`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`CSS-in-JS`}</strong>{` (styles written in `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`.css.ts`}</inlineCode>{` or `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`.css.js`}</inlineCode>{` files)`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`Webpack`}</strong>{` for bundling`}</li>
    </ul>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`Features:`}</strong></p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul">{`✅ Use native web components directly (`}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`<sp-button>`}</inlineCode>{`)`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`✅ Smaller bundle sizes (no React framework)`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`✅ Standards-based web components`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`✅ Great for developers who prefer vanilla JavaScript`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`✅ Spectrum design system built-in`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`❌ Requires learning Lit and web component patterns`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`❌ Less familiar if you're coming from React`}</li>
    </ul>
    <InlineAlert variant="info" slots="text" mdxType="InlineAlert" />
    <p>{`New to Lit? See the `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "../learn/how_to/tutorials/using-lit-typescript.md"
      }}>{`Using Lit & TypeScript Tutorial`}</a>{` for a complete guide to building add-ons with SWC templates.`}</p>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "what-are-react-templates"
    }}>{`What are React Templates?`}</h3>
    <p>{`React templates use the React framework for building UI.`}</p>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`React templates are characterized by:`}</strong></p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`React framework`}</strong>{` for component-based UI`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`React wrappers for Spectrum Web Components`}</strong>{` (`}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`<Button>`}</inlineCode>{`, `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`<Theme>`}</inlineCode>{` from `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`@swc-react`}</inlineCode>{`)`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`JSX syntax`}</strong>{` for writing components`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`Standard CSS`}</strong>{` files (`}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`.css`}</inlineCode>{`)`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`Webpack`}</strong>{` for bundling`}</li>
    </ul>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`Features:`}</strong></p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul">{`✅ Familiar React patterns (hooks, JSX, component lifecycle)`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`✅ Use Spectrum Web Components via React wrappers (`}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`@swc-react`}</inlineCode>{`)`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`✅ Huge React ecosystem and community`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`✅ Great for complex UIs with lots of state`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`✅ Spectrum design system built-in`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`❌ Larger bundle sizes (includes React framework)`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`❌ Requires learning React if you're new to it`}</li>
    </ul>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "the-real-difference"
    }}>{`The Real Difference`}</h3>
    <p>{`Both templates give you Spectrum Web Components, but they differ in `}<strong parentName="p">{`how you use them`}</strong>{`:`}</p>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`SWC Templates (Vanilla JS):`}</strong></p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-javascript"
      }}>{`// Use native web components directly
<sp-button size="m" @click=\${this._handleClick}>
    Create Rectangle
</sp-button>
`}</code></pre>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`React Templates:`}</strong></p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-jsx"
      }}>{`// Use React wrappers from @swc-react
<Button size="m" onClick={handleClick}>
    Create Rectangle
</Button>
`}</code></pre>
    <p>{`The choice is really about: `}<strong parentName="p">{`Do you prefer vanilla JavaScript/Lit or React?`}</strong></p>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "when-to-choose-swc-templates"
    }}>{`When to Choose SWC Templates`}</h3>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`Choose SWC templates if:`}</strong></p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul">{`✅ You want to use `}<strong parentName="li">{`Adobe's Spectrum design system`}</strong>{` out of the box`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`✅ You prefer `}<strong parentName="li">{`vanilla JavaScript`}</strong>{` over frameworks`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`✅ You're building a `}<strong parentName="li">{`simple to medium complexity`}</strong>{` UI`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`✅ You want `}<strong parentName="li">{`pre-built accessible components`}</strong>{` (`}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`<sp-button>`}</inlineCode>{`, `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`<sp-textfield>`}</inlineCode>{`, etc.)`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`✅ You're comfortable with `}<strong parentName="li">{`web components`}</strong>{` and Lit`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`✅ Your team doesn't already use React`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`✅ You want `}<strong parentName="li">{`smaller bundle sizes`}</strong></li>
    </ul>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`Examples of good SWC template use cases:`}</strong></p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Settings panels with forms`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Tools that use standard UI components (buttons, inputs, dropdowns)`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Add-ons that should match Adobe Express's look and feel`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Simple to medium complexity interfaces`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Projects where accessibility is a priority (Spectrum components are accessible by default)`}</li>
    </ul>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`Getting started with SWC:`}</strong></p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul"><a parentName="li" {...{
          "href": "../learn/how_to/tutorials/using-lit-typescript.md"
        }}>{`Using Lit & TypeScript Tutorial`}</a>{` - Step-by-step guide to building with SWC templates`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><a parentName="li" {...{
          "href": "../learn/how_to/tutorials/spectrum-workshop/index.md"
        }}>{`Using Adobe Spectrum Workshop`}</a>{` - Learn Spectrum Web Components`}</li>
    </ul>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "when-to-choose-react-templates"
    }}>{`When to Choose React Templates`}</h3>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`Choose React templates if:`}</strong></p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul">{`✅ You or your team `}<strong parentName="li">{`already knows React`}</strong></li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`✅ You're building a `}<strong parentName="li">{`complex UI`}</strong>{` with lots of state`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`✅ You need `}<strong parentName="li">{`rich component ecosystems`}</strong>{` (many React libraries available)`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`✅ You want `}<strong parentName="li">{`familiar development patterns`}</strong>{` (hooks, JSX, etc.)`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`✅ Your add-on has `}<strong parentName="li">{`multiple views or complex workflows`}</strong></li>
    </ul>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`Examples of good React template use cases:`}</strong></p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Multi-step wizards or workflows`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Data visualization tools`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Complex forms with validation`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Add-ons with multiple panels or views`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Projects where you want to use React UI libraries`}</li>
    </ul>

    <InlineAlert variant="info" slots="header, text" mdxType="InlineAlert" />
    <p>{`SWC and JS Libraries`}</p>
    <p>{`As a developer, you can use other libraries than Lit. For example, if you are a React developer, you can use `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "https://opensource.adobe.com/spectrum-web-components/using-swc-react/"
      }}><strong parentName="a">{`@swc-react`}</strong></a>{` to implement SWC components in your add-ons.`}</p>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "the-bottom-line"
    }}>{`The Bottom Line`}</h3>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`If you're new to add-on development:`}</strong></p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Start with `}<strong parentName="li">{`plain JavaScript`}</strong>{` template (no build) to learn the basics`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Move to `}<strong parentName="li">{`SWC JavaScript`}</strong>{` when you need a build tool but want simplicity`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Move to `}<strong parentName="li">{`React JavaScript`}</strong>{` if you need complex UI or already know React`}</li>
    </ul>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`If you're an experienced developer:`}</strong></p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Use `}<strong parentName="li">{`SWC templates`}</strong>{` for performance and simplicity`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Use `}<strong parentName="li">{`React templates`}</strong>{` if React is your comfort zone or you need complex UIs`}</li>
    </ul>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`Learn more about Spectrum:`}</strong></p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul"><a parentName="li" {...{
          "href": "../learn/how_to/tutorials/spectrum-workshop/index.md"
        }}>{`Using Adobe Spectrum Tutorial`}</a>{` - Complete workshop on Spectrum Web Components`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><a parentName="li" {...{
          "href": "../build/design/ux_guidelines/introduction.md"
        }}>{`UX Guidelines`}</a>{` - Design principles for Adobe Express add-ons`}</li>
    </ul>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "template-selection-guide"
    }}>{`Template Selection Guide`}</h2>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "quick-decision-tree"
    }}>{`Quick Decision Tree`}</h3>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-text"
      }}>{`Do you need to create/modify document content?
│
├─ NO → Do you need a complex UI?
│       │
│       ├─ NO → Use: javascript
│       │
│       └─ YES → Does your team use TypeScript?
│               │
│               ├─ NO → Use: react-javascript
│               └─ YES → Use: react-typescript
│
└─ YES → Do you need a complex UI?
        │
        ├─ NO → Does your team use TypeScript?
        │       │
        │       ├─ NO → Use: javascript-with-document-sandbox
        │       └─ YES → Use: swc-typescript-with-document-sandbox
        │
        └─ YES → Does your team use TypeScript?
                │
                ├─ NO → Use: react-javascript-with-document-sandbox
                └─ YES → Use: react-typescript-with-document-sandbox
`}</code></pre>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "recommendations-by-use-case"
    }}>{`Recommendations by Use Case`}</h3>
    <table>
      <thead parentName="table">
        <tr parentName="thead">
          <th parentName="tr" {...{
            "align": null
          }}>{`Use Case`}</th>
          <th parentName="tr" {...{
            "align": null
          }}>{`Recommended Template`}</th>
          <th parentName="tr" {...{
            "align": null
          }}>{`Why`}</th>
        </tr>
      </thead>
      <tbody parentName="table">
        <tr parentName="tbody">
          <td parentName="tr" {...{
            "align": null
          }}>{`Learning add-on development`}</td>
          <td parentName="tr" {...{
            "align": null
          }}><inlineCode parentName="td">{`javascript`}</inlineCode></td>
          <td parentName="tr" {...{
            "align": null
          }}>{`Simplest, no build tools`}</td>
        </tr>
        <tr parentName="tbody">
          <td parentName="tr" {...{
            "align": null
          }}>{`Simple utility (calculator, converter)`}</td>
          <td parentName="tr" {...{
            "align": null
          }}><inlineCode parentName="td">{`javascript`}</inlineCode></td>
          <td parentName="tr" {...{
            "align": null
          }}>{`No unnecessary complexity`}</td>
        </tr>
        <tr parentName="tbody">
          <td parentName="tr" {...{
            "align": null
          }}>{`Creating shapes/text in document`}</td>
          <td parentName="tr" {...{
            "align": null
          }}><inlineCode parentName="td">{`javascript-with-document-sandbox`}</inlineCode></td>
          <td parentName="tr" {...{
            "align": null
          }}>{`Adds document manipulation`}</td>
        </tr>

    <h3 {...{
      "id": "template-structure-examples"
    }}>{`Template Structure Examples`}</h3>
    <InlineAlert variant="info" slots="header, text1" mdxType="InlineAlert" />
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`Want to try SWC TypeScript templates?`}</strong></p>
    <p>{`Follow the `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "../learn/how_to/tutorials/using-lit-typescript.md"
      }}>{`Using Lit & TypeScript Tutorial`}</a>{` for a complete walkthrough of building an add-on with the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`swc-typescript-with-document-sandbox`}</inlineCode>{` template.`}</p>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "1-javascript-no-build-no-sandbox"
    }}>{`1. JavaScript (No Build, No Sandbox)`}</h4>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-text"
      }}>{`my-addon/
├── src/
│   ├── index.html
│   ├── index.js
│   └── manifest.json
└── tsconfig.json
`}</code></pre>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "2-javascript-with-document-sandbox-no-build"
    }}>{`2. JavaScript with Document Sandbox (No Build)`}</h4>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-text"
      }}>{`my-addon/
├── src/
│   ├── index.html
│   ├── manifest.json
│   ├── sandbox/
│   │   ├── code.js
│   │   └── tsconfig.json
│   └── ui/
│       ├── index.js
│       └── tsconfig.json
└── tsconfig.json
`}</code></pre>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "3-swc-javascript-with-document-sandbox-build"
    }}>{`3. SWC JavaScript with Document Sandbox (Build)`}</h4>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-text"
      }}>{`my-addon/
├── src/
│   ├── index.html
│   ├── manifest.json
│   ├── models/
│   │   └── DocumentSandboxApi.ts  # Type definitions
│   ├── sandbox/
│   │   ├── code.js
│   │   └── tsconfig.json
│   └── ui/
│       ├── components/
│       │   ├── App.css.js
│       │   └── App.js
│       ├── index.js
│       └── tsconfig.json
├── tsconfig.json
└── webpack.config.js
`}</code></pre>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "4-react-typescript-with-document-sandbox-build"
    }}>{`4. React TypeScript with Document Sandbox (Build)`}</h4>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-text"
      }}>{`my-addon/
├── src/
│   ├── index.html
│   ├── manifest.json
│   ├── models/
│   │   └── DocumentSandboxApi.ts  # Type definitions

│   │   ├── code.ts
│   │   └── tsconfig.json
│   └── ui/
│       ├── components/
│       │   ├── App.css
│       │   └── App.tsx
│       ├── index.tsx
│       └── tsconfig.json
├── tsconfig.json

`}</code></pre>

    <h2 {...{
      "id": "faqs"
    }}>{`FAQs`}</h2>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "q-why-does-my-manifest-say-documentsandbox-sandboxcodejs-but-the-template-has-codejs"
    }}>{`Q: Why does my manifest say `}<inlineCode parentName="h4">{`documentSandbox: 'sandbox/code.js'`}</inlineCode>{` but the template has `}<inlineCode parentName="h4">{`code.js`}</inlineCode>{`?`}</h4>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`A:`}</strong>{` It depends on whether you're using a build tool:`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`No-build (`}<inlineCode parentName="strong">{`javascript-with-document-sandbox`}</inlineCode>{`)`}</strong>{`: `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`"documentSandbox": "sandbox/code.js"`}</inlineCode>{` - points to source file`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`Build templates (all others)`}</strong>{`: `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`"documentSandbox": "code.js"`}</inlineCode>{` - webpack bundles to `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`dist/code.js`}</inlineCode></li>
    </ul>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "q-where-do-i-put-my-css-files"
    }}>{`Q: Where do I put my CSS files?`}</h4>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`A:`}</strong>{` Always in the iframe runtime, never in document sandbox. See the `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "#cssstyling"
      }}>{`CSS/Styling section`}</a>{` above for specific locations by template type.`}</p>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "q-why-cant-i-use-documentgetelementbyid-in-my-document-sandbox-code"
    }}>{`Q: Why can't I use `}<inlineCode parentName="h4">{`document.getElementById()`}</inlineCode>{` in my document sandbox code?`}</h4>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`A:`}</strong>{` The document sandbox has NO access to the DOM. See the `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "#document-manipulation-code-only-in-document-sandbox"
      }}>{`Document Manipulation Code section`}</a>{` above for the complete list of available and unavailable APIs.`}</p>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "q-do-i-need-both-indexjs-and-uiindexjs"
    }}>{`Q: Do I need both `}<inlineCode parentName="h4">{`index.js`}</inlineCode>{` and `}<inlineCode parentName="h4">{`ui/index.js`}</inlineCode>{`?`}</h4>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`A:`}</strong>{` No, it's one or the other:`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`Without document sandbox`}</strong>{`: Use `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`src/index.js`}</inlineCode></li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`With document sandbox`}</strong>{`: Use `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`src/ui/index.js`}</inlineCode>{` (code is organized into `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`ui/`}</inlineCode>{` folder)`}</li>
    </ul>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "q-why-do-i-have-tsconfigjson-if-im-using-javascript"
    }}>{`Q: Why do I have `}<inlineCode parentName="h4">{`tsconfig.json`}</inlineCode>{` if I'm using JavaScript?`}</h4>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`A:`}</strong>{` TypeScript config files provide IDE support (IntelliSense, autocomplete, error checking) even in JavaScript projects. You get better developer experience without needing to learn TypeScript.`}</p>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "q-can-i-share-utility-functions-between-ui-and-document-sandbox"
    }}>{`Q: Can I share utility functions between UI and document sandbox?`}</h4>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`A:`}</strong>{` Not directly - each environment is isolated. You have three options:`}</p>
    <ol>
      <li parentName="ol"><strong parentName="li">{`Duplicate the code`}</strong>{` in both `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`ui/`}</inlineCode>{` and `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`sandbox/`}</inlineCode>{` folders`}</li>
      <li parentName="ol"><strong parentName="li">{`Pass processed data`}</strong>{` between environments via the API proxy`}</li>
      <li parentName="ol"><strong parentName="li">{`Share TypeScript types/interfaces`}</strong>{` only (in `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`src/models/`}</inlineCode>{` or `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`src/types/`}</inlineCode>{`)`}</li>
    </ol>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "summary"
    }}>{`Summary`}</h2>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`Essential Takeaways:`}</strong></p>
    <ol>
      <li parentName="ol"><strong parentName="li">{`Only two templates have no build process:`}</strong>{` `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`javascript`}</inlineCode>{` and `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`javascript-with-document-sandbox`}</inlineCode></li>
      <li parentName="ol"><strong parentName="li">{`All other templates use webpack`}</strong>{` and output to `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`dist/`}</inlineCode></li>
      <li parentName="ol"><strong parentName="li">{`UI code always goes in iframe runtime`}</strong>{` (never in document sandbox)`}</li>
      <li parentName="ol"><strong parentName="li">{`CSS always goes in iframe runtime`}</strong>{` (never in document sandbox)`}</li>
      <li parentName="ol"><strong parentName="li">{`Document sandbox has limited browser APIs`}</strong>{` (no DOM, no `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`setTimeout()`}</inlineCode>{`, no `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`fetch()`}</inlineCode>{`)`}</li>
      <li parentName="ol"><strong parentName="li">{`Manifest paths differ`}</strong>{` between build and no-build templates`}</li>
      <li parentName="ol"><strong parentName="li">{`Start simple`}</strong>{` and upgrade as your add-on grows in complexity`}</li>
    </ol>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`When in doubt:`}</strong></p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul">{`UI/styling → iframe runtime`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Document manipulation → document sandbox`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Simple add-on → `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`javascript`}</inlineCode>{` template`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Need document access → add `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`-with-document-sandbox`}</inlineCode></li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Complex UI → add `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`react-`}</inlineCode></li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Type safety → add TypeScript variant`}</li>
    </ul>
